instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your hips as if sitting in a chair, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Go as low as you comfortably can, ideally until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position.
avoid: Lifting your heels off the ground.
citations: Schoenfeld, B. J. (2010). Squatting kinematics and kinetics and their application to exercise performance. Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research, 24(12), 3497-3506.

instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your eyes under the bar.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and lower it to your mid-chest.
Press the bar back up to the starting position.
avoid: Bouncing the bar off your chest.
Flaring your elbows out to 90 degrees.
Lifting your glutes off the bench.
citations: Lauver, J. D., Cayot, T. E., & Scheuermann, B. W. (2016). Influence of bench angle on upper extremity muscular activation during bench press exercise. European Journal of Sport Science, 30(3), 226-233.

instructions: Stand with your mid-foot under the barbell.
Bend over and grab the bar with a shoulder-width grip.
Bend your knees until your shins touch the bar.
Lift your chest up and straighten your lower back.
Stand up with the weight, keeping the bar close to your body.
avoid: Jerking the weight off the floor.
Leaning back excessively at the top.
citations: Farley, K. (1995). Analysis of the conventional deadlift. Strength & Conditioning Journal, 17(6), 55-57.
